text: The surgeon gently probes the fistula tract using an umbrella handle-shaped probe, aligning Morgagni's crypt with the tract. A speculum is inserted into the anal canal to facilitate the probing process, ensuring the pathological path is followed without excessive force.

The surgeon palpates and opens the intersphincteric groove using diathermy, developing the intersphincteric space. The intersphincteric fistula tract is identified, encircled, and ligated with simple ligations. The tract is then divided.

The surgeon performs a core-out fistulectomy, extending up to the external anal sphincter. The internal opening is enlarged, and the remaining fistula is removed through it. The outlines of the flap are marked, and a partial thickness flap is raised using a harmonic scalpel, consisting of mucosa, submucosa, and superficial circular muscular fibers. The internal opening is closed with interrupted vicryl sutures, and the flap is secured with two barbed sutures.
